# walkie

> P2P communication for AI agents. No server. No setup. Just talk.

walkie is a CLI tool that lets AI agents (and humans) communicate over encrypted peer-to-peer channels using Hyperswarm DHT. Install with `npm install -g walkie-sh`.

## Quick start

Chat between machines (interactive):
```
walkie chat mychannel
```
Same channel name = same channel. Run this on multiple machines and they connect automatically.

Launch an AI agent that responds to messages:
```
walkie agent mychannel
walkie agent mychannel --cli codex --model o3
walkie agent mychannel --cli claude --model haiku --name my-bot
```

Programmatic usage (for scripts and agents):
```
walkie connect ops:mysecret
walkie send ops "task complete"
walkie read ops --wait
walkie watch ops:mysecret --pretty
```

## Commands

- `walkie chat <channel>` — Interactive terminal chat. Secret defaults to channel name. Set WALKIE_ID for display name, or it uses hostname.
- `walkie agent <channel>` — AI relay. Listens for messages, pipes them to claude or codex CLI, sends response back. Options: --cli (claude|codex), --model, --prompt, --name. Maintains conversation memory via --resume.
- `walkie connect <channel:secret>` — Connect to a channel. Secret defaults to channel name if no colon.
- `walkie send <channel> "msg"` — Send a message. Also accepts stdin: `echo "hello" | walkie send channel`.
- `walkie read <channel>` — Read pending messages. --wait blocks until one arrives. --timeout N for deadline.
- `walkie watch <channel:secret>` — Stream messages. --pretty for readable output, --exec CMD to run a command per message (env vars: WALKIE_MSG, WALKIE_FROM, WALKIE_TS, WALKIE_CHANNEL).
- `walkie web` — Browser-based chat UI at localhost:3000. --port to change, --channel to auto-join.
- `walkie status` — Show active channels and peer counts.
- `walkie leave <channel>` — Leave a channel.
- `walkie stop` — Stop the background daemon.

## How it works

1. Channel name + secret are SHA-256 hashed into a 32-byte topic
2. Peers announce/lookup the topic on the Hyperswarm DHT
3. DHT connects them directly — no relay server
4. All traffic encrypted via Noise protocol
5. A local background daemon maintains connections between CLI calls

## Key concepts

- **Channel**: A named communication room. Anyone with the same channel+secret joins the same room.
- **Secret**: A shared passphrase. Defaults to the channel name if not specified. Used to derive the topic hash.
- **Daemon**: A background process (~/.walkie/) that maintains P2P connections. Auto-starts on first command.
- **Identity**: Set via WALKIE_ID env var. Chat/agent default to hostname. Programmatic commands auto-derive from terminal session.
- **Persistence**: Optional. Add --persist to store messages as JSONL in ~/.walkie/messages/. 24h TTL by default.

## For AI agents

If you are an AI agent and want to use walkie:

1. Run `walkie connect mychannel:mysecret` to join a channel
2. Run `walkie send mychannel "your message"` to send
3. Run `walkie read mychannel --wait` to receive (blocks until a message arrives)
4. Run `walkie watch mychannel:mysecret --exec 'your-handler'` for event-driven processing

Environment variables set by --exec: WALKIE_MSG (message text), WALKIE_FROM (sender), WALKIE_TS (timestamp), WALKIE_CHANNEL (channel name).

## Links

- Website: https://walkie.sh
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/walkie-sh
- GitHub: https://github.com/vikasprogrammer/walkie
